theory.title: Writing Skills: How to Write a Biography

sections.heading: 1. Tóm tắt lý thuyết
sections.content:
✍️ Cách viết tiểu sử

Cấu trúc bài viết tiểu sử:

  • Introduction (Mở bài): Giới thiệu người đó là ai, nổi tiếng về điều gì
  • Body Paragraph 1 (Thân bài 1): Thời thơ ấu, gia đình, giáo dục
  • Body Paragraph 2 (Thân bài 2): Sự nghiệp, thành tựu chính
  • Body Paragraph 3 (Thân bài 3): Thử thách đã vượt qua, phẩm chất
  • Conclusion (Kết bài): Di sản, ảnh hưởng, đánh giá chung

Ngôn ngữ sử dụng:

  • Tense: Chủ yếu dùng Past Simple (người đã mất) hoặc Present Perfect (người còn sống)
  • Linking words: First, Then, After that, However, Moreover, Finally
  • Time expressions: In 1990, At the age of 25, During his career
  • Descriptive language: Remarkable, outstanding, influential, dedicated

sections.heading: 2. Useful Expressions for Biography Writing
sections.content:
PurposeUseful Expressions
IntroductionX was born in... / X is known as... / X is one of the most... / X is widely regarded as...
Early lifeFrom an early age... / As a child... / He/She grew up in... / He/She showed interest in...
CareerHe/She began his/her career... / He/She achieved... / He/She is famous for... / He/She made significant contributions to...
ChallengesDespite facing... / He/She overcame... / Although... / He/She struggled with... but never gave up
QualitiesHe/She was known for... / He/She possessed... / What made him/her special was... / He/She demonstrated...
ConclusionHis/Her legacy... / He/She is remembered as... / Today, he/she continues to inspire... / His/Her impact on...

sections.heading: 3. Năm ví dụ mẫu về Biography Writing
sections.content:
Example 1: Short Biography (100 words)

Nelson Mandela - A Symbol of Peace

Nelson Mandela was born in South Africa in 1918. He became a lawyer and fought against apartheid, a system of racial discrimination. In 1962, he was imprisoned for 27 years but never gave up his beliefs. After his release in 1990, he worked for reconciliation and became South Africa's first Black president in 1994. Mandela received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993. He died in 2013, leaving a legacy of forgiveness and human rights. Today, he is remembered as one of the greatest leaders in history.

Example 2: Introduction Paragraph

Marie Curie is widely regarded as one of the most influential scientists in history. Born in Poland in 1867, she became the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields. Her groundbreaking research on radioactivity revolutionized science and medicine, and her determination in the face of discrimination opened doors for women in science worldwide.

Example 3: Body Paragraph (Early Life)

Steve Jobs was born in San Francisco in 1955 and was adopted by Paul and Clara Jobs. From an early age, he showed a keen interest in electronics and design. Although he dropped out of college after just six months, he continued to audit classes that interested him, including calligraphy, which later influenced Apple's elegant typography. At the age of 21, Jobs co-founded Apple Computer with Steve Wozniak in his parents' garage, marking the beginning of a revolutionary career in technology.

Example 4: Body Paragraph (Challenges)

Despite her remarkable achievements, Marie Curie faced numerous challenges throughout her life. As a woman in 19th-century Europe, she was denied access to higher education in Poland and had to move to Paris to pursue her studies. Even after winning her first Nobel Prize, she faced discrimination from the scientific community. Additionally, she worked in dangerous conditions without proper protection from radiation, which ultimately led to her death. However, these obstacles never deterred her from her passion for science.

Example 5: Conclusion Paragraph

Malala Yousafzai's legacy extends far beyond her Nobel Peace Prize. Today, she continues to inspire millions of young people around the world to stand up for their rights and pursue education. Through the Malala Fund, she has helped thousands of girls access education in developing countries. Her courage, determination, and unwavering commitment to her cause have made her a symbol of hope and resilience. As she once said, 'One child, one teacher, one book, and one pen can change the world,' and she has proven that age is no barrier to making a difference.

sections.heading: 4. Các dạng bài tập Writing
sections.content:
📝 Dạng bài tập Writing thường gặp

Dạng 1: Complete Biography

Viết một bài tiểu sử hoàn chỉnh (150-200 từ).

Tip: Lập outline trước, chia thành 4-5 đoạn rõ ràng.

Dạng 2: Paragraph Writing

Viết một đoạn văn về một khía cạnh cụ thể (early life, achievements, challenges).

Tip: Topic sentence + Supporting details + Concluding sentence.

Dạng 3: Sentence Transformation

Viết lại câu giữ nguyên nghĩa sử dụng từ cho sẵn.

Tip: Chú ý cấu trúc ngữ pháp và từ vựng.

Dạng 4: Gap-filling in Biography

Điền từ thích hợp vào chỗ trống trong bài tiểu sử.

Tip: Đọc cả đoạn để hiểu ngữ cảnh trước khi điền.

Dạng 5: Guided Writing

Viết tiểu sử dựa trên gợi ý cho sẵn (bullet points).

Tip: Mở rộng mỗi gợi ý thành 2-3 câu hoàn chỉnh.

sections.heading: 5. Writing Checklist
sections.content:
✅ Checklist trước khi nộp bài:

  • ☐ Có đủ 4-5 đoạn văn rõ ràng?
  • ☐ Mở bài giới thiệu người đó là ai?
  • ☐ Thân bài có thông tin về early life, career, achievements, challenges?
  • ☐ Kết bài có nói về legacy/impact?
  • ☐ Sử dụng đúng thì (Past Simple cho người đã mất)?
  • ☐ Có linking words để nối ý?
  • ☐ Từ vựng phong phú, không lặp lại?
  • ☐ Ngữ pháp chính xác?
  • ☐ Chính tả đúng?
  • ☐ Đủ số từ yêu cầu?

exercises.question: Which tense is mainly used for writing about someone who has died?
exercises.explanation: Past Simple được sử dụng chủ yếu khi viết về người đã qua đời.

exercises.question: Which is the best opening sentence for a biography?
options: Nelson Mandela is widely regarded as one of the greatest leaders.
exercises.explanation: Câu mở đầu tốt cần giới thiệu rõ ràng và ấn tượng về người đó.

exercises.question: Which linking word shows contrast?
exercises.explanation: 'However' được dùng để chỉ sự tương phản, đối lập.

exercises.question: Complete: 'Marie Curie _____ in Poland in 1867.'
exercises.explanation: 'Was born' là cấu trúc đúng ở thì quá khứ đơn.

exercises.question: Which expression is suitable for describing achievements?
exercises.explanation: 'Made significant contributions to' là cách diễn đạt tích cực về thành tựu.

exercises.question: What should a conclusion paragraph include?
exercises.explanation: Kết luận nên tóm tắt di sản và ảnh hưởng của người đó.

exercises.question: Which sentence shows overcoming challenges correctly?
options: Despite facing difficulties, she gave up.
Although she faced obstacles, she succeeded.
exercises.explanation: 'Although... she succeeded' thể hiện việc vượt qua thử thách.

exercises.question: Choose the best way to describe someone's qualities:
exercises.explanation: Mô tả cụ thể và chi tiết về phẩm chất làm bài viết sinh động hơn.

exercises.question: Which time expression is correct?
exercises.explanation: 'At the age of' là cụm từ đúng để chỉ tuổi.

exercises.question: What makes a biography interesting?
exercises.explanation: Ví dụ và chi tiết cụ thể làm bài tiểu sử hấp dẫn và sinh động.

exercises.question_text: Decide if these writing tips are good (True) or bad (False):
options: Use the same word repeatedly throughout the essay.
Include specific dates and facts to make the biography credible.
Write very long sentences without punctuation.
Use linking words to connect ideas smoothly.
exercises.explanation: a) False - Nên dùng từ vựng đa dạng. b) True - Thông tin cụ thể làm bài viết đáng tin. c) False - Câu quá dài khó đọc. d) True - Linking words giúp bài mạch lạc.

exercises.question_text: Are these suitable for a biography introduction? Mark True or False:
options: 'Marie Curie is widely regarded as a pioneering scientist.'
'I don't know much about this person.'
'Nelson Mandela was born in South Africa in 1918 and became one of the greatest leaders in history.'
exercises.explanation: a) False - Quá chung chung. b) True - Giới thiệu rõ ràng và ấn tượng. c) False - Không phù hợp. d) True - Cung cấp thông tin cụ thể và đánh giá.

exercises.question_text:
Read this biography excerpt and decide if the statements are True (T) or False (F):

'Steve Jobs was born in 1955. He co-founded Apple in 1976. In 1985, he left Apple. However, he returned in 1997 and made Apple successful again. He died in 2011.'
options: The paragraph uses Past Simple tense.
The paragraph is well-developed with details.
exercises.explanation: a) True - Dùng Past Simple. b) True - Có 'However'. c) True - Có nhiều năm cụ thể. d) False - Thiếu chi tiết, quá ngắn gọn.

exercises.passage:
Sample Biography for Analysis:

Malala Yousafzai: A Voice for Education

Malala Yousafzai is a Pakistani activist who has become a global symbol of the fight for girls' education. Born on July 12, 1997, in Mingora, Pakistan, Malala grew up in a region where the Taliban had banned girls from attending school.

From a young age, Malala was passionate about education. Encouraged by her father, who ran a school, she began speaking out about girls' rights to education. At just 11 years old, she started writing a blog for the BBC about life under Taliban rule. Her courage brought her international attention but also made her a target.

On October 9, 2012, when Malala was only 15, a Taliban gunman shot her in the head while she was on her school bus. Miraculously, she survived after receiving treatment in Pakistan and the United Kingdom. Rather than silencing her, this attack made her even more determined to fight for education rights.

After her recovery, Malala continued her activism with renewed vigor. In 2013, she co-founded the Malala Fund, an organization dedicated to ensuring girls worldwide can access 12 years of free, quality education. Her efforts earned her numerous awards, including the Nobel Peace Prize in 2014, making her the youngest Nobel laureate at age 17.

Today, Malala continues to inspire millions of young people around the world. She has shown that one person, regardless of age, can make a significant difference. Her famous words, 'One child, one teacher, one book, and one pen can change the world,' remind us of education's transformative power. Malala's legacy is not just about surviving an attack but about turning tragedy into a global movement for change.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of this biography?
options: To tell Malala's story and her fight for education

questions.question: Which paragraph discusses Malala's early life?
What tense is primarily used in this biography?
Which linking word shows contrast in the text?

exercises.explanation: Phân tích bài tiểu sử mẫu về Malala để hiểu cấu trúc và cách viết.

exercises.passage:
Writing Task Analysis:

Task: Write a biography of Albert Einstein (150-180 words)

Student's Answer:

Albert Einstein was a famous scientist. He was born in Germany in 1879. He was very smart. He studied physics. He made many discoveries. His most famous equation is E=mc². He won the Nobel Prize. He moved to America. He died in 1955. He was a great person. Everyone knows him. He changed science. His work was important. People still study his theories today.

Teacher's Comments:
- Too simple and repetitive
- Lacks specific details and examples
- No clear paragraph structure
- Limited vocabulary
- Missing information about challenges, qualities, and legacy
- Needs more sophisticated sentence structures
- Should include more context and explanation

Improved Version:

Albert Einstein, born in Germany in 1879, is widely regarded as one of the greatest physicists in history. Despite struggling with speech difficulties as a child, he showed exceptional talent in mathematics and physics from an early age.

Einstein's groundbreaking work revolutionized our understanding of the universe. In 1905, his 'miracle year,' he published four papers that changed physics forever, including his famous equation E=mc², which showed the relationship between mass and energy. His theory of relativity challenged Newton's laws and provided new insights into space, time, and gravity.

However, Einstein's life wasn't without challenges. As a Jewish scientist in Nazi Germany, he faced persecution and was forced to flee to the United States in 1933. Despite these difficulties, he continued his research and became a professor at Princeton University.

Einstein received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 and remained active in both science and humanitarian causes until his death in 1955. His legacy extends beyond his scientific achievements; he is remembered as a symbol of genius, creativity, and the power of imagination in scientific discovery.

questions.question: What is the main problem with the student's answer?
What does the improved version include that the student's doesn't?
How many paragraphs does the improved version have?
What challenge does the improved version mention?
options: He faced persecution as a Jewish scientist

questions.question: What makes the improved version better?
options: It has better structure, details, and vocabulary

exercises.explanation: So sánh bài viết yếu và bài viết tốt để học cách cải thiện kỹ năng viết tiểu sử.

exercises.question_text:
Complete the biography with appropriate words:

Mother Teresa was (1) _____ in Macedonia in 1910. She (2) _____ to India in 1929 and dedicated her life to helping the poor. She (3) _____ the Missionaries of Charity in 1950. Despite (4) _____ many challenges, she never gave up. She (5) _____ the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979. She (6) _____ in 1997, leaving a legacy of compassion.
exercises.correct:
1. born
2. moved/went
3. founded/established
4. facing
5. won/received
6. died
exercises.explanation: Hoàn thành tiểu sử ngắn về Mother Teresa với các động từ phù hợp.

exercises.question_text:
Fill in the blanks with suitable linking words:
[However, Moreover, First, Finally, Although, Therefore]

(1) _____, let me introduce Marie Curie. She was born in Poland. (2) _____, she faced discrimination as a woman. (3) _____, she never gave up. (4) _____, she was also a devoted mother. (5) _____, she won two Nobel Prizes. (6) _____, her legacy continues to inspire scientists today.
exercises.correct:
1. First
2. However/Moreover
3. However
4. Moreover
5. Therefore/Finally
6. Finally/Today
exercises.explanation: Sử dụng linking words để tạo sự mạch lạc cho bài viết.

exercises.question_text:
Rewrite these sentences using the words in brackets:

1. Marie Curie won two Nobel Prizes. (achievement)
→ Marie Curie's _____ was winning two Nobel Prizes.

2. She worked very hard. (dedication)
→ Her _____ to her work was remarkable.

3. She never stopped trying. (perseverance)
→ Her _____ helped her succeed.

4. She influenced many people. (impact)
→ She had a significant _____ on science.
exercises.explanation: Chuyển đổi câu sử dụng danh từ thay vì động từ để làm phong phú ngôn ngữ.

exercises.question_text:
Arrange these sentences in the correct order to form a coherent biography paragraph:

___ He received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993.
___ Nelson Mandela was born in South Africa in 1918.
___ After his release, he worked for reconciliation.
___ He was imprisoned for 27 years for fighting apartheid.
___ In 1994, he became South Africa's first Black president.
exercises.explanation: Thứ tự đúng: 1.Born 2.Imprisoned 3.Released 4.Nobel Prize 5.President (theo trình tự thời gian)

exercises.question:
Writing Task: Write a complete biography (180-200 words) about a person you admire. This could be:
- A family member
- A historical figure
- A contemporary leader
- A scientist, artist, or athlete

Your biography should include:
1. Introduction: Who they are and why they're significant
2. Early life and background
3. Major achievements and contributions
4. Challenges they overcame
5. Their qualities and characteristics
6. Conclusion: Their legacy and impact

Use:
- Past Simple tense (for completed actions)
- Present Perfect (for ongoing impact)
- Linking words (However, Moreover, Despite, Although, etc.)
- Descriptive vocabulary (remarkable, outstanding, influential, etc.)
- Specific examples and details
exercises.answer_guide:
Sample Answer:

My Grandmother: A Woman of Strength

The person I admire most is my grandmother, who has been a pillar of strength for our entire family. Born in a small village in 1945, she grew up during difficult times when opportunities for women were extremely limited.

Despite having little formal education, my grandmother possessed remarkable intelligence and determination. At the age of 20, she married my grandfather and moved to the city, where she had to adapt to a completely different way of life. She raised five children while also helping my grandfather run a small business. Her days were long and exhausting, but she never complained.

What makes my grandmother truly admirable is her resilience. When my grandfather passed away suddenly, she was left to support the family alone. Although many people expected her to give up, she worked even harder, taking on multiple jobs to ensure all her children could complete their education. All five of her children graduated from university, a testament to her sacrifice and dedication.

Moreover, my grandmother has always been generous and kind. Despite her own struggles, she regularly helped neighbors and relatives in need. She taught us that true wealth lies not in money but in compassion and family bonds.

Today, at 79, my grandmother continues to inspire our family with her wisdom and positive attitude. She has shown me that strength comes not from avoiding difficulties but from facing them with courage and grace. Her legacy of perseverance, kindness, and selflessness will continue to guide our family for generations to come.

(Word count: 247 words)

Key features of this biography:
✓ Clear structure with introduction, body, and conclusion
✓ Specific details and examples
✓ Mix of Past Simple and Present tenses
✓ Linking words (Despite, Although, Moreover, Today)
✓ Descriptive vocabulary (remarkable, resilience, testament, legacy)
✓ Personal connection and emotional impact
✓ Shows both achievements and challenges
✓ Concludes with lasting impact
exercises.explanation: Bài viết tiểu sử hoàn chỉnh cần có cấu trúc rõ ràng, nội dung cụ thể, ngôn ngữ phong phú, và thể hiện được tầm quan trọng của người được viết. Hãy luyện tập viết nhiều lần và nhờ giáo viên/bạn bè góp ý để cải thiện.
